items.headline: Get Your Latest NBA News From RealGM's Basketball Wiretap
items.blurb: Get Your Latest NBA News From RealGM's Basketball Wiretap — Get all the latest news and happenings from around the NBA with RealGM's Basketball Wiretap.

items.headline: Cade Cunningham matches Pistons icon Isiah Thomas as Detroit ties series
items.blurb: Cade Cunningham matches Pistons icon Isiah Thomas as Detroit ties series — Putting on his proverbial cape when called upon, Cade Cunningham led the Detroit Pistons to a much-needed Game 2 win on Thursday night.

items.headline: Gilgeous-Alexander outduels Brooks, Thunder beat Suns in Game 2
items.blurb: Gilgeous-Alexander outduels Brooks, Thunder beat Suns in Game 2 — Shai Gilgeous-Alexander had 37 points and nine assists, and the Oklahoma City Thunder defeated the Phoenix Suns 120-107 on Wednesday night to take a 2-0 lead in their first-round Western Conference playoff series.

items.headline: Spurs' Keldon Johnson named Sixth Man of the Year
items.blurb: Spurs' Keldon Johnson named Sixth Man of the Year — For the second time in three days, a Spurs player has won a major award.

items.headline: Thunder's Game 2 win over Suns overshadowed by Jalen Williams injury
items.blurb: Thunder's Game 2 win over Suns overshadowed by Jalen Williams injury — The Oklahoma City Thunder took a 2-0 lead over the Phoenix Suns following Wednesday's 120-107 win, but it was not without a key injury and poor execution down the stretch.

items.headline: Thunder’s Jalen Williams injures leg in Game 2 vs. Suns
items.blurb: Thunder’s Jalen Williams injures leg in Game 2 vs. Suns — Oklahoma City Thunder guard/forward Jalen Williams left in the third quarter of Wednesday night’s playoff game against the Phoenix Suns with a left hamstring injury.

items.headline: Draymond Green admits he may not return to the Warriors
items.blurb: Draymond Green admits he may not return to the Warriors — Things are changing for the Warriors, and Green admitted he is unsure of his future with the team.

items.headline: SGA has some fun with Brooks after shaking him for pull-up jumper
items.blurb: SGA has some fun with Brooks after shaking him for pull-up jumper — Watch as Oklahoma City Thunder superstar Shai Gilgeous-Alexander has some fun at the expense of a fellow Canadian, taunting Phoenix Suns swingman Dillon Brooks after nailing a jumper despite some stingy defence by Brooks.

items.headline: Pistons’ Cunningham leaves Banchero in his tracks with nasty crossover
items.blurb: Pistons’ Cunningham leaves Banchero in his tracks with nasty crossover — Watch as Cade Cunningham crosses Banchero over with a nasty move and blows past him to get the easy bucket.

items.headline: Steve Kerr drops a major hint about his next job
items.blurb: Steve Kerr drops a major hint about his next job — Steve Kerr may be following the lead of former Golden State Warriors colleague Bob Myers.

items.headline: Wendell Carter Jr., Jalen Duren's rivalry continues in Magic-Pistons series
items.blurb: Wendell Carter Jr., Jalen Duren's rivalry continues in Magic-Pistons series — When Wendell Carter, Jr. delivered a hard foul to Jalen Duren during the third quarter of the Detroit Pistons' 98-83 win over the Orlando Magic, there was speculation online that the move was about more than basketball.

items.headline: Adam Silver Supports Grizzlies Playing Select Games In Nashville While Backing Memphis Long-Term
items.blurb: Adam Silver Supports Grizzlies Playing Select Games In Nashville While Backing Memphis Long-Term — NBA Commissioner Adam Silver expressed confidence in the Memphis Grizzlies' long-term viability Wednesday while acknowledging he would welcome a greater franchise presence in Nashville, responding directly to recent remarks by LeBron James suggesting the team relocate. items.headline: Raptors Parent Company Plans $18.25 Billion Sports Entity After MLSE Buyout
items.blurb: Raptors Parent Company Plans $18.25 Billion Sports Entity After MLSE Buyout — Rogers Communications, majority owner of the Toronto Raptors through its 75% stake in Maple Leaf Sports & Entertainment, announced plans Wednesday to complete its full acquisition of MLSE and consolidate its sports holdings into a single entity valued at more than $18.25 billion. CEO Tony Staffieri confirmed on the compa

items.headline: Aspiration Founder Cooperated With NBA's Kawhi Leonard Salary Cap Investigation
items.blurb: Aspiration Founder Cooperated With NBA's Kawhi Leonard Salary Cap Investigation — Joseph Sanberg, the Aspiration co-founder facing federal sentencing April 27, provided substantial cooperation to the NBA's ongoing salary cap circumvention investigation involving Kawhi Leonard and the Los Angeles Clippers, the league's lead investigator confirmed in a court letter. items.headline: Keldon Johnson Wins 2025-26 NBA Sixth Man Of The Year Award
items.blurb: Keldon Johnson Wins 2025-26 NBA Sixth Man Of The Year Award — San Antonio Spurs guard-forward Keldon Johnson has won the 2025-26 NBA Sixth Man of the Year Award , earning the John Havlicek Trophy after a standout season off the bench. Johnson averaged 13.2 points and 5.4 rebounds in 23.3 minutes per game, becoming the only player in the league to appear in all 82 games as a reserve. He shot 51.9%

items.headline: Pistons blow out Magic in Game 2 with dominant third quarter
items.blurb: Pistons blow out Magic in Game 2 with dominant third quarter — Through the first six quarters of their first-round series, the Orlando Magic were as good or better than the top-seeded Detroit Pistons. Then the third quarter of Game 2 happened.

items.headline: Steve Kerr Meeting With Media Agents Ahead Of Warriors' Decision
items.blurb: Steve Kerr Meeting With Media Agents Ahead Of Warriors' Decision — Steve Kerr has been meeting with media agents as his Golden State Warriors contract approaches expiration, with broadcasting, front office work, and a return to coaching all remaining possibilities, sources told Front Office Sports. Kerr has not yet settled on a next step. A spokesperson for his coaching agency, Priority Sports, de

items.headline: Poeltl must be better to give Raptors a chance to turn series around
items.blurb: Poeltl must be better to give Raptors a chance to turn series around — The Toronto Raptors have a Jakob Poeltl problem. It’s been ebbing in and out of sight for most of the season. But with the playoff spotlight in full glare, it’s impossible to miss.

items.headline: Patrick Ewing explains why he would still dominate modern NBA era
items.blurb: Patrick Ewing explains why he would still dominate modern NBA era — The 1990s basketball era gets remembered for one thing above all else: physicality. Games were slower, grittier and built around power in the paint. That reputation created a narrative that dominant big men from that decade wouldn't survive in today's game. Patrick Ewing doesn't buy it.

items.headline: The 'Most 7.0 FT-per-game NBA seasons' quiz
items.blurb: The 'Most 7.0 FT-per-game NBA seasons' quiz — How many of the NBA players to make at least 7.0 free-throws-per-game in three or more seasons can you name in five minutes?

items.headline: Darius Acuff Jr. Declares For 2026 NBA Draft
items.blurb: Darius Acuff Jr. Declares For 2026 NBA Draft — Arkansas guard Darius Acuff Jr. announced Wednesday on ESPN's "NBA Today" that he is entering the 2026 NBA Draft. The 6-foot-3 point guard is currently ranked the sixth-best prospect available, per ESPN's Jeremy Woo. "With that being said, I'm going to be declaring for the 2026 NBA draft," Acuff said. Acuff had a record-setting freshman campaign with

items.headline: ‘Can’t wait for his podcast’: Adelman on McDaniels criticism of Nuggets defence
items.blurb: ‘Can’t wait for his podcast’: Adelman on McDaniels criticism of Nuggets defence — Denver Nuggets head coach David Adelman fires back at Minnesota Timberwolves’ Jaden McDaniels after he criticized the team’s defensive efforts, backing the performance of his players early in the postseason.

items.headline: Draymond Green makes big prediction about Steve Kerr's future with Warriors
items.blurb: Draymond Green makes big prediction about Steve Kerr's future with Warriors — Green believes Kerr has coached his last game for the Golden State Warriors.

items.headline: Raptors’ Ingram confident he’ll ‘find a rhythm’ and quell shooting woes
items.blurb: Raptors’ Ingram confident he’ll ‘find a rhythm’ and quell shooting woes — Toronto Raptors forward Brandon Ingram meets with the media and addresses his recent struggles and how he is confident that he will turn his offensive struggles around while also being confident in the group as a whole.

items.headline: ‘I’ll fight with this guy any day’: Rajakovic has faith in Ingram
items.blurb: ‘I’ll fight with this guy any day’: Rajakovic has faith in Ingram — Toronto Raptors head coach Darko Rajakovic meets with the media and discusses the confidence that he and the roster have in the struggling Brandon Ingram as the team heads into Game 3 against the Cleveland Cavaliers.

items.headline: The '30-point, 0-assist playoff games' quiz
items.blurb: The '30-point, 0-assist playoff games' quiz — How many of the 67 players since the NBA/ABA merger in 1976-77 to score 30 points without dishing out an assist in a postseason game can you name in eight minutes?

items.headline: Raptors’ Immanuel Quickley ‘getting better every day,’ says coach
items.blurb: Raptors’ Immanuel Quickley ‘getting better every day,’ says coach — The Toronto Raptors’ starting point guard seems closer to making his playoff debut.

items.headline: ‘We want to play with pace’: Temple says offensive mindset is to push
items.blurb: ‘We want to play with pace’: Temple says offensive mindset is to push — Toronto Raptors forward Garrett Temple joins The Raptors Show and delves into the team’s offensive mindset going into Game 3 against the Cleveland Cavaliers in wanting to get turnovers and push the ball in transition offensively.

items.headline: Warriors may have a surprise candidate to replace Steve Kerr
items.blurb: Warriors may have a surprise candidate to replace Steve Kerr — After four championships and six trips to the NBA Finals, Kerr could step down as head coach.

items.headline: The 'Youngest 30-10 NBA playoff games' quiz
items.blurb: The 'Youngest 30-10 NBA playoff games' quiz — How many of the players to score at least 30 points and haul in a least 10 rebounds in an NBA playoff game before turning 23 years old can you name in six minutes?

items.headline: Should Raptors’ Barnes get exclusive assignment on Cavs’ star guards?
items.blurb: Should Raptors’ Barnes get exclusive assignment on Cavs’ star guards? — Former Toronto Raptors guard Alvin Williams joins The Raptors Show and discusses the Raptors’ defensive philosophy against the Cleveland Cavaliers and whether Scottie Barnes should be the primary defender on either Donovan Mitchell or James Harden.

items.headline: France to host men’s World Cup in 2031, Japan gets 2030 women’s tournament
items.blurb: France to host men’s World Cup in 2031, Japan gets 2030 women’s tournament — Victor Wembanyama will have a chance to play a home World Cup in 2031.

items.headline: Jimmy Butler may not return in 2026-27
items.blurb: Jimmy Butler may not return in 2026-27 — The Golden State Warriors signed Jimmy Butler to an extension last season in hopes of getting two seasons with him alongside Steph Curry. They may end up only getting half a season.

items.headline: Shai Gilgeous-Alexander: Nears double-double in win
items.blurb: Shai Gilgeous-Alexander: Nears double-double in win — Gilgeous-Alexander supplied 37 points (13-25 FG, 2-5 3Pt, 9-9 FT), nine assists, five rebounds and one steal across 38 minutes in Wednesday's 120-107 Game 2 win over the Suns in the first round of the playoffs. Visit RotoWire.com for more analysis on this update.

items.headline: Jalen Williams: Exits early Wednesday
items.blurb: Jalen Williams: Exits early Wednesday — Williams exited Wednesday's 120-107 Game 2 win over the Suns in the third quarter and did not return due to an apparent left hamstring injury, finishing with 19 points (7-11 FG, 2-3 3Pt, 3-5 FT), four assists, one rebound and one steal in 23 minutes. Visit RotoWire.com for more analysis on this update.

items.headline: Austin Reaves Closer To Returning Than Luka Doncic
items.blurb: Austin Reaves Closer To Returning Than Luka Doncic — The Los Angeles Lakers have taken a 2-0 series lead over the Houston Rockets, despite being shorthanded. Now, Luka Doncic and Austin Reaves are starting the process of ramping up for a return. Reaves is closer to being able to play than Doncic. "The sense around the Lakers is that Austin Reaves is actually the one that's further along than Luka

items.headline: LeBron James outduels Kevin Durant as Lakers take 2-0 series lead
items.blurb: LeBron James outduels Kevin Durant as Lakers take 2-0 series lead — Tuesday night, LeBron James and Kevin Durant met in the postseason for the first time since 2018. Durant started hot but James and the Los Angeles Lakers closed stronger in their 101-94 win over the Houston Rockets.

items.headline: Jalen Williams: Exits to locker room
items.blurb: Jalen Williams: Exits to locker room — Williams exited to the locker room in the third quarter of Wednesday's Game 2 against the Suns due to an apparent left hamstring injury, Brandon Rahbar of DailyThunder.com reports. Visit RotoWire.com for more analysis on this update.

items.headline: Marcus Smart Dominates Kevin Durant, Lakers Take 2-0 Series Lead Over Rockets
items.blurb: Marcus Smart Dominates Kevin Durant, Lakers Take 2-0 Series Lead Over Rockets — Marcus Smart delivered a standout two-way performance Tuesday, holding Kevin Durant in check while scoring 25 points to lead the Los Angeles Lakers to a 101-94 Game 2 victory over the Houston Rockets. Durant, returning from a right knee injury that sidelined him for Game 1, finished with 23 points. However, he shot jus

items.headline: Cade Cunningham: Powers Detroit to victory
items.blurb: Cade Cunningham: Powers Detroit to victory — Cunningham contributed 27 points (11-19 FG, 1-6 3Pt, 4-8 FT), 11 assists, six rebounds, one block and one steal across 37 minutes in Wednesday's 98-83 Game 2 win over the Magic in the first round of the playoffs. Visit RotoWire.com for more analysis on this update.

items.headline: Mark Williams: Ruled out Wednesday
items.blurb: Mark Williams: Ruled out Wednesday — Williams (foot) has been ruled out for Wednesday's Game 2 against the Thunder, Kellan Olson of ArizonaSports.com reports. Visit RotoWire.com for more analysis on this update.

items.headline: Colin Cowherd floats intriguing NBA playoff team for Billy Donovan's next job
items.blurb: Colin Cowherd floats intriguing NBA playoff team for Billy Donovan's next job — Billy Donovan is no longer the head coach of the Chicago Bulls. After six seasons, he has decided to step down. That doesn't mean that Donovan is stepping away from coaching, though.

items.headline: Scoot Henderson On Scoring 31 Points In Blazers' Game 2 Win: 'Attack, Get Downhill'
items.blurb: Scoot Henderson On Scoring 31 Points In Blazers' Game 2 Win: 'Attack, Get Downhill' — Scoot Henderson scored 31 points as the Portland Trail Blazers defeated the San Antonio Spurs 106-103 to win Game 2 and even the series. Henderson, who did not score 30 points in the regular season, finished 11-of-17 from the field. "I'm so blessed to even play in a situation like this," Henderson said. "Playoffs

items.headline: Napheesa Collier Out Until Early June
items.blurb: Napheesa Collier Out Until Early June — Minnesota Lynx star Napheesa Collier will be out until early June after undergoing ankle surgery on March 24, the team announced on Tuesday. Collier also had surgery on her right ankle in January. Collier, who signed a one-year supermax contract to return to the Lynx, will be an unrestricted free agent next season. Cade Cunningham matches Pistons icon Isiah Thomas as Detroit ties series — Putting on his proverbial cape when called upon, Cade Cunningham led the Detroit Pistons to a much-needed Game 2 win on Thursday night.

Gilgeous-Alexander outduels Brooks, Thunder beat Suns in Game 2 — Shai Gilgeous-Alexander had 37 points and nine assists, and the Oklahoma City Thunder defeated the Phoenix Suns 120-107 on Wednesday night to take a 2-0 lead in their first-round Western Conference playoff series.
Spurs' Keldon Johnson named Sixth Man of the Year — For the second time in three days, a Spurs player has won a major award.

Thunder's Game 2 win over Suns overshadowed by Jalen Williams injury — The Oklahoma City Thunder took a 2-0 lead over the Phoenix Suns following Wednesday's 120-107 win, but it was not without a key injury and poor execution down the stretch.

Thunder’s Jalen Williams injures leg in Game 2 vs. Suns — Oklahoma City Thunder guard/forward Jalen Williams left in the third quarter of Wednesday night’s playoff game against the Phoenix Suns with a left hamstring injury.
Draymond Green admits he may not return to the Warriors — Things are changing for the Warriors, and Green admitted he is unsure of his future with the team.

SGA has some fun with Brooks after shaking him for pull-up jumper — Watch as Oklahoma City Thunder superstar Shai Gilgeous-Alexander has some fun at the expense of a fellow Canadian, taunting Phoenix Suns swingman Dillon Brooks after nailing a jumper despite some stingy defence by Brooks.
Pistons’ Cunningham leaves Banchero in his tracks with nasty crossover — Watch as Cade Cunningham crosses Banchero over with a nasty move and blows past him to get the easy bucket.
